format_phases.notes: Split throughout: LTX-2.3 UI or sample video on top; face-bottom with gaming-LED studio
Host fills full frame; no split — emphasis beat 'whether you have a PC, a MacBook, a Mac Mini'
Returns to split; B-roll swaps rapidly through LTX generation samples
Cluster of 5 scene cuts in ~1s — rapid montage of LTX sample videos (flash-cut pairs)
Back to split; B-roll continues with LTX samples
Split with double-caption tracks: top caption on B-roll (sometimes baked into the LTX sample), bottom retro-pixel caption in the face region
Full-frame face; giant 'Ltx' product-name logo-style caption overlaid center

events.label: Top: LTX-2.3 app home with 'Next-generation quality, now supporting portrait' hero + 'Recent Projects' grid (bear, rooster, cowboy, old-man, nightclub-man thumbnails). Bottom: host in Mets cap, black hoodie, gaming-studio w/ red-lit monitors

events.text: You no longer have to pay 50 bucks a month for AI video generation.

events.style: white all-caps, pixel/8-bit retro font (Press Start 2P-style), slight drop shadow, center-frame over split divider

events.description: Cut B-roll to stylized anime cel-shaded illustration (yellow/red close-up of a face w/ red eye-glow, anime style)

events.label: Anime-style cel-shaded face with red color grading, yellow goggles + red eye-glow — LTX 2.3 sample 'A NEW MODEL' beat

events.text: A new model, LTX 2.3, can run locally on consumer GPUs.

events.description: 3 rapid scene cuts at 4.42, 4.88, 5.26 — swapping B-roll samples (LTX showreel 'consumer GPUs' beat)

events.label: Fast succession of LTX samples (various stylistic generations) — signals 'multiple outputs' conceptually
Full-frame face close-up, Mets cap + hoodie, hand gestures prominent. Gaming-studio background (warm green/red LED bokeh left; red-lit monitor panel right)

events.text: So whether you have a PC, a MacBook, a Mac Mini,
for
events.style: italic serif (Cormorant/Playfair italic), white, center-frame mid-height, no pill, standalone word

events.text: you can have run a production grade video model
for free
for free directly in your machine, which gives you a lot of advantages.

events.description: Back to split-screen mode, top swaps to LTX node-graph UI (ComfyUI-style)

events.label: ComfyUI-like node graph screenshot showing LTX 2.3 pipeline (Prepare Frames, CLIP Text Encode, LTXVLatent, LTX Preprocess, LTXSampler, LTX Sampling Process, LTX KSA nodes with wired connections) — demonstrates local-rig configurability

events.description: B-roll swap to burning-house cinematic LTX sample with 'Ltx' overlay

events.label: Cinematic wheat-field + burning farm house at dusk, 'Ltx' logo overlay floating in smoke (LTX sample with baked-in brand mark)

events.text: It's not living on someone's server somewhere.

events.description: B-roll swap to misty forest with three hooded figures (LTX sample)

events.label: Three hooded figures walking through a foggy moonlit forest with glowing blue-green particles, cinematic grade — LTX sample. Caption 'It's multimodal' is baked into the LTX sample video

events.text: You don't have to worry about subscription,
creep, restrictions, or throttling,
but let me tell you the really exciting part.

events.label: Extreme close-up of the host (same Mets cap + hoodie, identical framing to bottom face) — suggesting the B-roll is a *match-cut to a higher-res or LTX-restyled version of the host himself*

events.description: Five scene cuts in ~0.85s (23.27, 23.48, 23.69, 23.90, 24.11) = high-speed LTX showreel montage — 'most downloaded and highest benchmarking open source video model ever' beat

events.text: LTX 2, which I covered before in this video,

events.label: Cloaked red-cape warrior with axe standing in burning medieval ruin village — LTX sample. Caption 'AND HIGHEST' overlaid in pixel retro font over the bottom face

events.text: was the most downloaded and highest benchmarking
open source video model ever.

events.label: LTX sample continuing (caption 'VIDEO MODEL' in retro pixel font visible)

events.text: LTX 2.3 improves on that model in every single way

events.label: Another extreme close-up of face B-roll — 'AND WHAT WE GET' retro pixel caption over the bottom face region

events.text: based on community feedback.
And what we get is a nearly state-of-the-art model
where you have full control and ownership over your data and IP,

events.label: LTX sample: fur-clad warrior swinging a big axe in a dusty rocky-cave battle — caption 'you can use it without restriction.' baked into the LTX sample itself

events.text: LTX 2.3
events.style: retro pixel font, white with shadow, center-frame

events.label: LTX sample: young woman at riverside evening light — caption 'And it's completely free' baked into LTX sample (regular subtitle-style). Bottom caption 'OVER YOUR DATA' in retro pixel

events.text: which is a really big deal for creators and entrepreneurs,
because this can power the products that you build.

events.label: LTX sample: denim-jacket woman driving a vintage pickup truck through rural highway, hands on wheel — baked caption 'with open source in mind.' Bottom caption 'THIS' retro pixel
Last LTX sample holding during CTA setup

events.text: So if you want to try LTX 2.3 and run it on your local machine,

events.description: Cut to full-frame face for CTA, giant 'Ltx' product-name logo overlay

events.label: Full-frame face close-up. Giant 'Ltx' bold-sans white letters center-frame (product-name emphasis) + smaller retro pixel 'I'LL SEND YOU' caption below — stacked captioning

events.text: comment LTX, and I'll send you the model
with full instructions on how to set it up.

style_summary.concept: product-showcase reel where B-roll IS the product's own output (LTX 2.3 video samples) rather than a UI demo
signature_elements: Persistent split-screen B-roll-top + face-bottom as default layout (not just hook-only like Nick 1)
B-rolls are the product's OWN sample outputs (LTX 2.3 generations) — not UI demos. Genius when the product is generative: every B-roll doubles as proof
Two parallel caption tracks can stack simultaneously — pixel/retro-font caption on the face region + subtitle-style caption on the B-roll top (sometimes baked into the LTX sample itself)
Italic-serif emphasis captions ('for free', 'for') for narrative pivots — reminiscent of Devin's emphasis slams but lighter-touch typographic treatment
Rapid flash-cut montage at narrative intensity peaks (5 cuts in ~1s at 23.27s)
Giant product-name logo overlay ('Ltx') on the final CTA shot — acts like a brand lock-up ring-off
Gaming/creator-studio backdrop — dark, warm+green LED practicals, red-lit monitor panels — conveys 'power user rig'
NY Mets cap as consistent visual identity + wedding-band hand-gestures
Full-frame face only at emphasis beats (7.05-12.14s, 50.68-end); otherwise perma-split
style_summary.caption_philosophy: Dual-track: retro pixel caption as the main narrator track + italic-serif emphasis at pivots. B-rolls often have their OWN baked subtitle tracks from LTX samples that the host synchronizes to his VO.
style_summary.color_accent: No single brand-accent — LTX-sample warm/cinematic grades dominate (orange fire, green mist, red warrior cloaks). Brand comes from font + layout consistency, not hue.
style_summary.contrast_with_other_styles: Only style in our set that uses persistent split-screen as default and where B-roll IS the product's own output. Cut density higher than any other (1/1.8s vs. next-highest 1/2.5s VC 1). Emphasis captions are lighter italic serif (vs. Devin's heavy gradient slams).
